## Deep Dive Investigation Report: Scratchpad Data Model

### 1. Scratchpad Database Schema

**File:** `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/crates/panopt-core/src/db.rs`

**Current Schema Version:** `SCHEMA_VERSION = 8` (line 12)

**Scratchpad Table Definition (V1, line 32-38):**
```sql
CREATE TABLE scratchpads (
    project_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id),
    id         INTEGER NOT NULL,
    title      TEXT    NOT NULL,
    body       TEXT    NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (project_id, id)
);
```

**Schema Evolution:**

- **V2 (lines 41-84):** Added foundational todo infrastructure; scratchpads untouched
- **V3 (lines 86-107):** Added agent/process roster; scratchpads untouched  
- **V4 (lines 109-143, apply_v4 fn):** Added timestamps to scratchpads
  ```sql
  ALTER TABLE scratchpads ADD COLUMN created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '';
  ALTER TABLE scratchpads ADD COLUMN updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '';
  ```
  - Backfills existing scratchpads with `datetime('now')` (line 139-142)
  - Uses `add_column_if_missing()` guard (line 126-136) to handle dev-database drift
  
- **V5 (lines 145-168, apply_v5 fn):** **Unified ID Counter**
  ```sql
  ALTER TABLE projects ADD COLUMN next_id INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1;
  UPDATE projects SET next_id = MAX(next_todo_id, next_scratchpad_id, next_roster_id);
  ALTER TABLE projects DROP COLUMN next_todo_id;
  ALTER TABLE projects DROP COLUMN next_scratchpad_id;
  ALTER TABLE projects DROP COLUMN next_roster_id;
  ```
  - Scratchpads now draw from shared `projects.next_id`, same as todos and processes
  - Pre-V5 collisions (same id in todos and scratchpads tables) persist as-is
  - Only fresh allocations post-migration are guaranteed globally unique

- **V6 (lines 170-232, apply_v6 fn):** Split roster into config/instance layers; scratchpads untouched

- **V7 (lines 297-310, apply_v7 fn):** Added soft-delete column
  ```sql
  ALTER TABLE scratchpads ADD COLUMN deleted_at TEXT;
  ```
  - Nullable, so no backfill needed
  - All read paths filter `WHERE deleted_at IS NULL` (line 306)

- **V8 (lines 312-322, apply_v8 fn):** Added tags support
  ```sql
  ALTER TABLE scratchpads ADD COLUMN tags TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]';
  ```
  - JSON array string, mirroring todos (V2 line 53: `ALTER TABLE todos ADD COLUMN tags TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'`)
  - Shared tag vocabulary with todos (todo #61, documented in V8 comment)
  - Uses `add_column_if_missing()` guard again for drift safety (line 321)

**Current Full Scratchpad Schema (post-V8):**
| Column | Type | Constraints | Purpose |
|--------|------|-------------|---------|
| project_id | INTEGER | NOT NULL, FK projects(id) | Project scope |
| id | INTEGER | NOT NULL, PK with project_id | Unique within project, drawn from `projects.next_id` |
| title | TEXT | NOT NULL | User-visible name |
| body | TEXT | NOT NULL | Main content |
| tags | TEXT | NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]' | JSON array of tag strings |
| created_at | TEXT | NOT NULL DEFAULT '' | SQLite `datetime('now')` UTC |
| updated_at | TEXT | NOT NULL DEFAULT '' | SQLite `datetime('now')` UTC, bumped on every mutation |
| deleted_at | TEXT | NULL | Soft-delete timestamp; NULL = live |

**ID Counter:** `projects.next_id` (unified across todos, scratchpads, agent_tools, processes) — see V5 migration.

---

### 2. Scratchpad Domain Model (Rust Structs)

**File:** `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/crates/panopt-core/src/model.rs` (lines 16-43)

```rust
/// A shared, append-oriented note identified by a stable numeric id.
///
/// The `id` (not the `title`) is the durable handle and the projected filename,
/// so renaming a scratchpad never moves its file. The id is unique within its
/// project and restarts at 1 in each project.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
pub struct Scratchpad {
    pub id: u64,
    pub title: String,
    pub body: String,
    /// Free-text labels drawn from the project's tag vocabulary, shared with
    /// todos (todo #61): [`crate::Store::tags_list`] returns the union across
    /// both kinds, so a tag set on one surface is offered up on the other.
    pub tags: Vec<String>,
    pub created_at: String,
    pub updated_at: String,
}

/// A set of optional edits to a [`Scratchpad`], applied by
/// [`crate::Store::scratchpad_update`].
///
/// Each `None` field leaves that attribute untouched; each `Some` replaces it.
#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]
pub struct ScratchpadPatch {
    pub title: Option<String>,
    pub body: Option<String>,
    pub tags: Option<Vec<String>>,
}
```

**Struct Details:**
- No status/priority/type field (unlike `Todo` which has `status: TodoStatus` and `priority: Priority`)
- Tags are shared vocabulary with todos (V8 adds union to both)
- Timestamps are string format (SQLite `datetime('now')` = `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`)
- `ScratchpadPatch` for partial updates (used by `scratchpad_update()`)

---

### 3. Scratchpad vs Todo Data Model Differences

**File:** `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/crates/panopt-core/src/model.rs` (lines 45-173)

**Todo Struct (for comparison):**
```rust
pub struct Todo {
    pub id: u64,
    pub title: String,
    pub body: String,
    pub status: TodoStatus,        // ← ENUM: Open, InProgress, Backlog, Draft, Completed, NotDone
    pub priority: Priority,        // ← ENUM: High, Medium, Low
    pub assignee: String,          // ← Plain string name
    pub tags: Vec<String>,         // ← SHARED with scratchpads
    pub blockers: Vec<u64>,        // ← Cross-todo dependencies
    pub comments: Vec<TodoComment>,// ← Per-todo comment thread
    pub created_at: String,
    pub updated_at: String,
    pub completed_at: Option<String>, // ← Set only when status=Completed
}

pub enum TodoStatus {
    #[default]
    Open,
    InProgress,
    Backlog,
    Draft,
    Completed,
    NotDone,
}

pub enum Priority {
    High,
    #[default]
    Medium,
    Low,
}
```

**TodoPatch (for comparison):**
```rust
pub struct TodoPatch {
    pub title: Option<String>,
    pub body: Option<String>,
    pub status: Option<TodoStatus>,
    pub priority: Option<Priority>,
    pub assignee: Option<String>,
    pub tags: Option<Vec<String>>,
}
```

**ID Counter Sharing:**
Both todos and scratchpads draw from the same unified `projects.next_id` counter (V5 migration). This means a scratchpad with id `#5` and a todo with id `#5` cannot coexist in the same project.

**Key Differences:**
| Feature | Todo | Scratchpad |
|---------|------|-----------|
| Status | Enum (6 variants) | None |
| Priority | Enum (3 variants) | None |
| Assignee | Plain string | None |
| Comments | Full side table | None |
| Blockers | Cross-linking table | None |
| Type/Kind field | No | No |
| Tags | Yes (V2) | Yes (V8) |
| Timestamps | created_at, updated_at, completed_at | created_at, updated_at |

---

### 4. Scratchpad MCP Tool Parameters

**File:** `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/crates/panopt-tool-surface/src/params.rs` (lines 16-76) and `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/crates/panopt-tool-surface/src/lib.rs` (lines 118-171)

**Tool Definitions & Parameter Structs:**

| Tool | Parameters |
|------|------------|
| **scratchpad_create** | `title: String` (required) |
| **scratchpad_list** | (no arguments) → returns `{id, title}` pairs |
| **scratchpad_search** | `query?: Option<String>`, `tags?: Option<Vec<String>>` (AND semantics) |
| **scratchpad_append** | `scratchpad_id: u64` (required), `content: String` (required) |
| **scratchpad_read** | `scratchpad_id: u64` (required) → returns body string |
| **scratchpad_get** | `scratchpad_id: u64` (required) → returns full `{id, title, body, tags, created_at, updated_at}` |
| **scratchpad_update** | `scratchpad_id: u64` (required), `title?: Option<String>`, `body?: Option<String>`, `tags?: Option<Vec<String>>` |
| **scratchpad_delete** | `scratchpad_id: u64` (required) |
| **scratchpad_tags_list** | (no arguments) → returns union of all todo+scratchpad tags |

**Source Code References:**
- `ScratchpadCreateArgs` (line 17-20): title only
- `ScratchpadUpdateArgs` (line 43-58): title, body, tags; all optional
- `ScratchpadSearchArgs` (line 67-76): query, tags
- Other structs (lines 23-64): straightforward parameter containers

Note: No `status`, `priority`, `type`, or `kind` field is exposed in any MCP tool.

---

### 5. Filesystem Projection

**File:** `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/crates/panopt-core/src/projection.rs`

**Directory Structure:**
```
.panopt/
  scratchpad/          ← directory for scratchpad files
    1.md              ← per-scratchpad file
    2.md
    3.md
  scratchpads.md      ← index file listing all scratchpads
```

**Path Functions (lines 42-55):**
```rust
fn scratchpad_dir(ws: &Path) -> PathBuf {
    panopt_dir(ws).join("scratchpad")
}

fn scratchpad_path(ws: &Path, id: u64) -> PathBuf {
    scratchpad_dir(ws).join(format!("{id}.md"))
}

fn scratchpads_index_path(ws: &Path) -> PathBuf {
    panopt_dir(ws).join("scratchpads.md")
}
```

**Atomic Write Pattern (lines 81-108):**
```rust
/// Atomically replace `target` with `contents`.
/// The temp file is created in `target`'s own directory, which guarantees it is
/// on the same filesystem - a precondition for `rename` to be atomic.
fn atomic_write(target: &Path, contents: &str) -> io::Result<()> {
    // ...
    let seq = TMP_SEQ.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
    let tmp = parent.join(format!(".{file_name}.tmp.{}.{seq}", std::process::id()));
    
    match fs::write(&tmp, contents).and_then(|()| fs::rename(&tmp, target)) {
        Ok(()) => Ok(()),
        Err(e) => {
            let _ = fs::remove_file(&tmp);
            Err(e)
        }
    }
}
```
- Write to `.{filename}.tmp.{pid}.{seq}` in same directory
- Atomic rename over target
- Cleanup on failure

**Per-Scratchpad File Rendering (lines 184-200):**
```rust
/// Render a scratchpad as a `---` frontmatter block of tags + timestamps, the
/// title as an H1, then its body verbatim. The frontmatter mirrors
/// `render_todo_md` so every per-item file in the projection carries the same
/// structured-then-prose shape. Empty `tags` renders as `tags: ` (no special
/// case), exactly like todos do.
pub(crate) fn render_scratchpad_md(pad: &Scratchpad) -> String {
    let mut out = String::from("---\n");
    out.push_str(&format!("tags: {}\n", pad.tags.join(", ")));
    out.push_str(&format!("created: {}\n", pad.created_at));
    out.push_str(&format!("updated: {}\n", pad.updated_at));
    out.push_str("---\n\n");
    out.push_str(&format!("# {}\n\n", pad.title));
    out.push_str(&pad.body);
    if !pad.body.is_empty() && !pad.body.ends_with('\n') {
        out.push('\n');
    }
    out
}
```

**Example Scratchpad Markdown File:**
```markdown
---
tags: notes, review
created: 2026-05-23 18:05:00
updated: 2026-05-23 18:05:21
---

# Project Notes

This is the body content as raw markdown...
```

**Index File Rendering (lines 203-220):**
```rust
/// Render the scratchpad index: one line per scratchpad, each linking to that
/// scratchpad's own file. `pads` is `(id, title, updated_at)`, id-ascending;
/// the `updated_at` is embedded in the trailing label so every append changes
/// the rendered bytes, which is how the cockpit sidebar (a 1s file poller)
/// notices the change.
pub(crate) fn render_scratchpads_index_md(pads: &[(u64, String, String)]) -> String {
    let mut out = String::from("# Scratchpads\n\n");
    if pads.is_empty() {
        out.push_str("_(no scratchpads)_\n");
        return out;
    }
    for (id, title, updated) in pads {
        out.push_str(&format!(
            "- [#{id}](scratchpad/{id}.md) {title} - updated {updated}\n",
        ));
    }
    out
}
```

**Example Index File:**
```markdown
# Scratchpads

- [#1](scratchpad/1.md) Notes - updated 2026-05-23 18:05:21
- [#2](scratchpad/2.md) Meeting Agenda - updated 2026-05-23 18:12:00
```

**Projection Functions (lines 409-443):**
- `project_scratchpad(ws, pad)` — writes one `<id>.md` file (line 410-411)
- `project_scratchpads_index(ws, pads)` — rewrites index and sweeps deleted files (lines 418-443)
  - Deletion is soft-delete in DB, but the orphaned `.panopt/scratchpad/<id>.md` file is swept here

---

## Summary: Renaming "Scratchpad" to "Notes" and Adding a "Type" Field

### Renaming: Cosmetic vs. Schema Implications

**API/UI Layer (PURELY COSMETIC):**
- Rename `scratchpad_*` MCP tool names to `notes_*` (or keep backwards-compat aliases)
- Rename Rust struct names: `Scratchpad` → `Notes`, `ScratchpadPatch` → `NotesPatch`
- Update projection filenames: `.panopt/scratchpad/` → `.panopt/notes/`, `scratchpads.md` → `notes.md`
- Update documentation and comments

**Database Layer (OPTIONAL):**
- **Current:** Table is named `scratchpads`
- **Option A (recommend):** Keep table name `scratchpads` in schema, only change external naming. Simpler migration path.
- **Option B:** Rename table to `notes` via V9 migration using `ALTER TABLE scratchpads RENAME TO notes;` — requires schema bump but is straightforward.

**Verdict:** The rename is **NOT inherently a DB migration**; it can be a purely presentational change. If you rename the table, you'll need V9 migration (~5 lines).

---

### Adding a "Type" Field

**Low-effort approach (NO MIGRATION):**
- Store type as a JSON field in frontmatter or tags
- Extend `ScratchpadPatch` and params to accept `type: Option<String>`
- No schema change needed

**Production approach (REQUIRES MIGRATION):**

Add a new V9 migration:
```rust
fn apply_v9(conn: &Connection) -> Result<(), rusqlite::Error> {
    add_column_if_missing(conn, "scratchpads", "type", "TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'note'")?;
    Ok(())
}
```

Then update the schema as you would for todos:
- Add `type: TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'note'` column
- Create enum `ScratchpadType { Note, Reference, Research, ... }` with `.as_str()` / `.parse()` methods (mirrors `Priority` & `TodoStatus`)
- Update `Scratchpad` struct: add `pub type: ScratchpadType`
- Update `ScratchpadPatch` struct: add `pub type: Option<ScratchpadType>`
- Update `ScratchpadUpdateArgs`: add optional `type` string parameter
- Update `render_scratchpad_md()` to include `type: {type}` in frontmatter

**Schema change footprint:**
- V9 migration: 10–15 lines
- model.rs: add enum (5–10 lines), update struct (2 lines)
- state.rs `fetch_scratchpad()`: parse type column (3–5 lines)
- state.rs `scratchpad_update()`: serialize type (2 lines)
- projection.rs `render_scratchpad_md()`: emit type field (1 line)
- params.rs `ScratchpadUpdateArgs`: add optional field (5 lines)

**Cost: Moderate but straightforward.** The pattern already exists (copy from `Priority` enum and todo-handling code).

---

### Other Observations

1. **Shared ID Counter:** Scratchpads and todos draw from the same `projects.next_id` (since V5). A "type" field could disambiguate on read, but the `id_kind` MCP tool already does this (file: `panopt-tool-surface/src/lib.rs` line 352–360).

2. **Unified Tag Vocabulary:** Scratchpads and todos share tags (V8). A type field would not change this.

3. **No Hard Constraints:** Unlike todos (status enum, priority enum), scratchpads are currently untyped — a type field would be optional by default, backward-compatible.

4. **Filesystem Already Namespaced:** Files are at `.panopt/scratchpad/{id}.md` and `.panopt/scratchpads.md` (note singular vs. plural). The projection code handles it cleanly, so moving files during a rename is safe via atomic write.

```rust
/// A shared, append-oriented note identified by a stable numeric id.
///
/// The `id` (not the `title`) is the durable handle and the projected filename,
/// so renaming a scratchpad never moves its file. The id is unique within its
/// project and restarts at 1 in each project.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
pub struct Scratchpad {
    pub id: u64,
    pub title: String,
    pub body: String,
    /// Free-text labels drawn from the project's tag vocabulary, shared with
    /// todos (todo #61): [`crate::Store::tags_list`] returns the union across
    /// both kinds, so a tag set on one surface is offered up on the other.
    pub tags: Vec<String>,
    pub created_at: String,
    pub updated_at: String,
}

/// A set of optional edits to a [`Scratchpad`], applied by
/// [`crate::Store::scratchpad_update`].
///
/// Each `None` field leaves that attribute untouched; each `Some` replaces it.
#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]
pub struct ScratchpadPatch {
    pub title: Option<String>,
    pub body: Option<String>,
    pub tags: Option<Vec<String>>,
}
```

- Tags are shared vocabulary with todos (V8 adds union to both)
- Timestamps are string format (SQLite `datetime('now')` = `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`)
- `ScratchpadPatch` for partial updates (used by `scratchpad_update()`)

**Atomic Write Pattern (lines 81-108):**
```rust
/// Atomically replace `target` with `contents`.
/// The temp file is created in `target`'s own directory, which guarantees it is
/// on the same filesystem - a precondition for `rename` to be atomic.
fn atomic_write(target: &Path, contents: &str) -> io::Result<()> {
    // ...
    let seq = TMP_SEQ.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
    let tmp = parent.join(format!(".{file_name}.tmp.{}.{seq}", std::process::id()));
    
    match fs::write(&tmp, contents).and_then(|()| fs::rename(&tmp, target)) {
        Ok(()) => Ok(()),
        Err(e) => {
            let _ = fs::remove_file(&tmp);
            Err(e)
        }
    }
}
```
- Write to `.{filename}.tmp.{pid}.{seq}` in same directory
- Atomic rename over target
- Cleanup on failure

**Per-Scratchpad File Rendering (lines 184-200):**
```rust
/// Render a scratchpad as a `---` frontmatter block of tags + timestamps, the
/// title as an H1, then its body verbatim. The frontmatter mirrors
/// `render_todo_md` so every per-item file in the projection carries the same
/// structured-then-prose shape. Empty `tags` renders as `tags: ` (no special
/// case), exactly like todos do.
pub(crate) fn render_scratchpad_md(pad: &Scratchpad) -> String {
    let mut out = String::from("---\n");
    out.push_str(&format!("tags: {}\n", pad.tags.join(", ")));
    out.push_str(&format!("created: {}\n", pad.created_at));
    out.push_str(&format!("updated: {}\n", pad.updated_at));
    out.push_str("---\n\n");
    out.push_str(&format!("# {}\n\n", pad.title));
    out.push_str(&pad.body);
    if !pad.body.is_empty() && !pad.body.ends_with('\n') {
        out.push('\n');
    }
    out
}
```

```rust
/// Render the scratchpad index: one line per scratchpad, each linking to that
/// scratchpad's own file. `pads` is `(id, title, updated_at)`, id-ascending;
/// the `updated_at` is embedded in the trailing label so every append changes
/// the rendered bytes, which is how the cockpit sidebar (a 1s file poller)
/// notices the change.
pub(crate) fn render_scratchpads_index_md(pads: &[(u64, String, String)]) -> String {
    let mut out = String::from("# Scratchpads\n\n");
    if pads.is_empty() {
        out.push_str("_(no scratchpads)_\n");
        return out;
    }
    for (id, title, updated) in pads {
        out.push_str(&format!(
            "- [#{id}](scratchpad/{id}.md) {title} - updated {updated}\n",
        ));
    }
    out
}
```

- [#1](scratchpad/1.md) Notes - updated 2026-05-23 18:05:21

## Summary: Renaming "Scratchpad" to "Notes" and Adding a "Type" Field

### Renaming: Cosmetic vs. Schema Implications

**API/UI Layer (PURELY COSMETIC):**
- Rename `scratchpad_*` MCP tool names to `notes_*` (or keep backwards-compat aliases)
- Rename Rust struct names: `Scratchpad` → `Notes`, `ScratchpadPatch` → `NotesPatch`
- Update projection filenames: `.panopt/scratchpad/` → `.panopt/notes/`, `scratchpads.md` → `notes.md`
- Update documentation and comments

**Database Layer (OPTIONAL):**
- **Current:** Table is named `scratchpads`
- **Option A (recommend):** Keep table name `scratchpads` in schema, only change external naming. Simpler migration path.
- **Option B:** Rename table to `notes` via V9 migration using `ALTER TABLE scratchpads RENAME TO notes;` — requires schema bump but is straightforward.

**Verdict:** The rename is **NOT inherently a DB migration**; it can be a purely presentational change. If you rename the table, you'll need V9 migration (~5 lines).

---

### Adding a "Type" Field

**Low-effort approach (NO MIGRATION):**
- Store type as a JSON field in frontmatter or tags
- Extend `ScratchpadPatch` and params to accept `type: Option<String>`
- No schema change needed

**Production approach (REQUIRES MIGRATION):**

Add a new V9 migration:
```rust
fn apply_v9(conn: &Connection) -> Result<(), rusqlite::Error> {
    add_column_if_missing(conn, "scratchpads", "type", "TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'note'")?;
    Ok(())
}
```

Then update the schema as you would for todos:
- Add `type: TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'note'` column
- Create enum `ScratchpadType { Note, Reference, Research, ... }` with `.as_str()` / `.parse()` methods (mirrors `Priority` & `TodoStatus`)
- Update `Scratchpad` struct: add `pub type: ScratchpadType`
- Update `ScratchpadPatch` struct: add `pub type: Option<ScratchpadType>`
- Update `ScratchpadUpdateArgs`: add optional `type` string parameter
- Update `render_scratchpad_md()` to include `type: {type}` in frontmatter

**Schema change footprint:**
- V9 migration: 10–15 lines
- model.rs: add enum (5–10 lines), update struct (2 lines)
- state.rs `fetch_scratchpad()`: parse type column (3–5 lines)
- state.rs `scratchpad_update()`: serialize type (2 lines)
- projection.rs `render_scratchpad_md()`: emit type field (1 line)
- params.rs `ScratchpadUpdateArgs`: add optional field (5 lines)

**Cost: Moderate but straightforward.** The pattern already exists (copy from `Priority` enum and todo-handling code).

---

### Other Observations

1. **Shared ID Counter:** Scratchpads and todos draw from the same `projects.next_id` (since V5). A "type" field could disambiguate on read, but the `id_kind` MCP tool already does this (file: `panopt-tool-surface/src/lib.rs` line 352–360).

2. **Unified Tag Vocabulary:** Scratchpads and todos share tags (V8). A type field would not change this.

3. **No Hard Constraints:** Unlike todos (status enum, priority enum), scratchpads are currently untyped — a type field would be optional by default, backward-compatible.

4. **Filesystem Already Namespaced:** Files are at `.panopt/scratchpad/{id}.md` and `.panopt/scratchpads.md` (note singular vs. plural). The projection code handles it cleanly, so moving files during a rename is safe via atomic write.

input.content:
# Plan: Rename "Scratchpads" → "Notes" (todo #79)

## Context

Todo #79 asks to rename the **Scratchpad** concept to **Notes**. The motivation:
"scratchpad" implies ephemerality the user doesn't want, and it's tedious to type.
The concept already serves as durable, append-oriented, shared text between agents
and humans — "Notes" fits better.

**Decisions (confirmed with user):**
- **Name:** `Notes`. MCP tools become singular `note_*` (matching `todo_*`).
- **Type field:** *Deferred.* The todo's open question ("should they have a type?
  note/plan/memory/inter-agent") is out of scope here — track as a follow-up todo.
- **Depth:** *Full rename.* Rust structs, DB table, state methods, projection paths,
  plugin, and docs all move to "note". No lingering "scratchpad" in code.
- **Compat:** *Hard rename.* No `scratchpad_*` aliases; only `note_*` exists.

This is a large but mechanical rename. The risky pieces — the DB table and the
filesystem projection paths — are isolated and handled explicitly below.

## Naming map

| Old | New |
|-----|-----|
| MCP tools `scratchpad_create/list/search/append/read/get/update/delete/tags_list` | `note_create/list/search/append/read/get/update/delete/tags_list` |
| Tool arg field `scratchpad_id` | `note_id` |
| DB table `scratchpads` | `notes` |
| Struct `Scratchpad`, `ScratchpadPatch` | `Note`, `NotePatch` |
| Error `CoreError::ScratchpadNotFound` | `CoreError::NoteNotFound` |
| Projection dir `.panopt/scratchpad/<id>.md` | `.panopt/note/<id>.md` |
| Projection index `.panopt/scratchpads.md` (heading `# Scratchpads`) | `.panopt/notes.md` (heading `# Notes`) |
| Plugin `Mode::Scratchpads`, pane mode `"scratchpads"` | `Mode::Notes`, `"notes"` |
| Viewer `Target::Scratchpad/NewScratchpad/ScratchpadList`, `Content::ScratchpadForm` | `Target::Note/NewNote/NoteList`, `Content::NoteForm` |
| Form `ScratchpadForm`, `ScratchpadFormAction` | `NoteForm`, `NoteFormAction` |
| Delete-gate `Kind::Scratchpad` | `Kind::Note` |

`projects.next_id` is already a unified counter (V5) — **no counter change needed**.

## Changes by crate

### 1. `panopt-core` (data layer — do this first)

- **`db.rs`** — the only schema change:
  - Bump `SCHEMA_VERSION` 8 → 9.
  - Add `apply_v9` performing `ALTER TABLE scratchpads RENAME TO notes;`, wired into
    `migrate` behind `if version < 9`. Guard for dev-database drift the way the
    existing migrations do (`add_column_if_missing` is the precedent): only rename

attachment.content:
- verify-all: Run the full check + clippy sweep across the workspace AND the WASM plugin (panopt-zellij). Use after non-trivial edits, before declaring work done, or when asked to verify the repo builds cleanly. Captures the dual-target dance (workspace builds for host, panopt-zellij builds only for wasm32-wasip1) so you don't have to remember the right manifest-path/target each time.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

109	/// Version 4: timestamps on scratchpads (todo #76).
110	///
111	/// Adds `created_at` and `updated_at` to `scratchpads` so every mutation -
112	/// including append - can bump `updated_at` and the projected index line can
113	/// surface it. That makes the cockpit sidebar visibly refresh on every
114	/// scratchpad change, the same way every todo mutation already changes the
115	/// todo index line through its status/priority fields. The backfill mirrors
116	/// the v1->v2 todo backfill: any pre-existing scratchpad lands with both
117	/// timestamps set to `datetime('now')`.
118	///
119	/// The `ALTER`s run through [`add_column_if_missing`] rather than a raw
120	/// `execute_batch`, so a database stuck at `user_version = 3` whose
121	/// `scratchpads` table already carries the v4 columns - the transitional
122	/// state of any machine that ran an in-development v4 binary before the
123	/// `user_version` bump landed - upgrades cleanly instead of failing on
124	/// "duplicate column name". The backfill is unconditionally safe.
125	fn apply_v4(conn: &Connection) -> Result<(), rusqlite::Error> {
126	    add_column_if_missing(
127	        conn,
128	        "scratchpads",
129	        "created_at",
130	        "TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''",
131	    )?;
132	    add_column_if_missing(
133	        conn,
134	        "scratchpads",
135	        "updated_at",
136	        "TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''",
137	    )?;
138	    conn.execute_batch(
139	        "UPDATE scratchpads
140	            SET created_at = datetime('now'), updated_at = datetime('now')
141	          WHERE created_at = '';",
142	    )
143	}

145	/// Version 5: unify per-resource id counters into one global `next_id` (todo #16).
146	///
147	/// Before V5 every project row carried three independent counters -
148	/// `next_todo_id`, `next_scratchpad_id`, `next_roster_id` - so the same number
149	/// could be handed out as a todo *and* a scratchpad *and* a roster entry. V5
150	/// replaces them with a single `next_id` so a `#N` reference points to exactly
151	/// one resource type forever. Existing rows are left in place: SQLite's
152	/// `(project_id, id)` primary keys are per-table, so any collision in the
153	/// pre-V5 data persists as-is and only the fresh allocations after the
154	/// migration are guaranteed unique.
155	///
156	/// The seed for `next_id` is `max(old three counters)`, which is the lowest
157	/// value safe against every pre-existing id in any of the three tables. The
158	/// three old columns are then dropped.
159	fn apply_v5(conn: &Connection) -> Result<(), rusqlite::Error> {
160	    conn.execute_batch(
161	        "ALTER TABLE projects ADD COLUMN next_id INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1;
162	         UPDATE projects
163	            SET next_id = MAX(next_todo_id, next_scratchpad_id, next_roster_id);
164	         ALTER TABLE projects DROP COLUMN next_todo_id;
165	         ALTER TABLE projects DROP COLUMN next_scratchpad_id;
166	         ALTER TABLE projects DROP COLUMN next_roster_id;",
167	    )
168	}
169	
170	/// Version 6: split the hybrid `roster` table into a config layer
171	/// (`agent_tools`) and an instance layer (`processes`) (todo #27).
172	///
173	/// A V5 roster row was both a config (name, command, cwd) and the implicit
174	/// single instance of that config; liveness was derived from Zellij pane state,
175	/// never stored. V6 mirrors Solo's two-layer model: `agent_tools` holds the
176	/// durable configurations, `processes` holds per-project instances and carries
177	/// a nullable FK back to its source tool plus nullable lifecycle columns
178	/// (`pid`, `status`, `agent_state`, `last_seen`) so a follow-up that owns
179	/// process spawn can populate them without another migration.
180	///
181	/// Both new tables keep drawing ids from the unified `projects.next_id`
182	/// counter introduced by V5, so a `#N` reference still resolves to exactly one
183	/// row across todos, scratchpads, agent_tools, and processes.
184	///
185	/// Migration of existing rows: `kind='agent'` becomes an `agent_tools` row
186	/// (configuration), and `kind='command'` / `kind='terminal'` become
187	/// `processes` rows (instances - the only thing the pre-split roster ever was
188	/// for those kinds). Ids are preserved verbatim since both new tables share
189	/// the same `next_id` sequence the originals were drawn from.
190	fn apply_v6(conn: &Connection) -> Result<(), rusqlite::Error> {
191	    conn.execute_batch(
192	        "CREATE TABLE agent_tools (
193	             project_id   INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id),
194	             id           INTEGER NOT NULL,
195	             name         TEXT    NOT NULL,
196	             display_name TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
197	             command      TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
198	             cwd          TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
199	             tool_type    TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'agent',
200	             enabled      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
201	             position     INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
202	             created_at   TEXT    NOT NULL,
203	             PRIMARY KEY (project_id, id)
204	         );
205	         CREATE TABLE processes (
206	             project_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id),
207	             id            INTEGER NOT NULL,
208	             kind          TEXT    NOT NULL CHECK (kind IN ('agent','command','terminal')),
209	             name          TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
210	             display_name  TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
211	             command       TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
212	             cwd           TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
213	             position      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
214	             agent_tool_id INTEGER,
215	             pid           INTEGER,
216	             status        TEXT,
217	             agent_state   TEXT,
218	             last_seen     TEXT,
219	             created_at    TEXT    NOT NULL,
220	             PRIMARY KEY (project_id, id)
221	         );
222	         INSERT INTO agent_tools
223	             (project_id, id, name, display_name, command, cwd, tool_type, enabled, position, created_at)
224	             SELECT project_id, id, name, display_name, command, cwd, 'agent', 1, position, created_at
225	               FROM roster WHERE kind = 'agent';
226	         INSERT INTO processes
227	             (project_id, id, kind, name, display_name, command, cwd, position, created_at)
228	             SELECT project_id, id, kind, name, display_name, command, cwd, position, created_at
229	               FROM roster WHERE kind IN ('command','terminal');
230	         DROP TABLE roster;",
231	    )
232	}

234	/// Add `column` to `table` only if it is not already there. Used by migrations
235	/// that may collide with a transitional dev-database state where the column
236	/// landed before its `user_version` bump did. `decl` is the type+constraint
237	/// text after the column name, exactly as it would appear in an `ALTER TABLE`.
238	///
239	/// `column` and `table` are fixed in-crate identifiers, not caller input, so
240	/// interpolating them into the SQL is safe.
241	fn add_column_if_missing(
242	    conn: &Connection,
243	    table: &str,
244	    column: &str,
245	    decl: &str,
246	) -> Result<(), rusqlite::Error> {
247	    let mut stmt = conn.prepare(&format!("PRAGMA table_info({table})"))?;
248	    let mut rows = stmt.query([])?;
249	    while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {
250	        let existing: String = row.get(1)?;
251	        if existing == column {
252	            return Ok(());
253	        }
254	    }
255	    drop(rows);
256	    drop(stmt);
257	    conn.execute_batch(&format!("ALTER TABLE {table} ADD COLUMN {column} {decl};"))
258	}
259	
260	/// Bring `conn` up to [`SCHEMA_VERSION`], creating tables on a fresh database.
261	///
262	/// Idempotent: a database already at the current version is left untouched.
263	/// Each step is gated on the stored `user_version`, so a v1 database upgrades
264	/// in place and a fresh one runs every step in order to the same end state.
265	pub(crate) fn migrate(conn: &Connection) -> Result<(), rusqlite::Error> {
266	    let version: i64 = conn.pragma_query_value(None, "user_version", |r| r.get(0))?;
267	    if version < 1 {
268	        conn.execute_batch(SCHEMA_V1)?;
269	    }
270	    if version < 2 {
271	        conn.execute_batch(SCHEMA_V2)?;
272	    }
273	    if version < 3 {
274	        conn.execute_batch(SCHEMA_V3)?;
275	    }
276	    if version < 4 {
277	        apply_v4(conn)?;
278	    }
279	    if version < 5 {
280	        apply_v5(conn)?;
281	    }
282	    if version < 6 {
283	        apply_v6(conn)?;
284	    }
285	    if version < 7 {
286	        apply_v7(conn)?;
287	    }
288	    if version < 8 {

attachment.snippet:
1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## Workspace layout
6	
7	Five crates under `crates/`:
8	
9	- `panopt-core` — transport-agnostic state, persistence (SQLite), filesystem projection. **Must not depend on `rmcp`, `axum`, or `tokio`** — this boundary is the reason core is reusable. Verify with `cargo tree -p panopt-core` if you add a dep.
10	- `panopt-tool-surface` — the single declaration of the MCP tool surface: `Parameters<T>` types plus a `TOOL_SURFACE` table (name, description, schema-generating function). Both `panoptd` and `panopt`'s `_mcp-proxy` register their tool routes from this table, so the proxy can answer `tools/list` without panoptd being up. **Must not depend on `rmcp`, `axum`, `tokio`, or `panoptd`** — verify with `cargo tree -p panopt-tool-surface`.
11	- `panoptd` — MCP daemon over Streamable HTTP. Defaults to `127.0.0.1:7600`; bind is configurable via `--host` and every request is gated by a bearer token at `~/.local/share/panopt/token` (`Authorization: Bearer <token>` or `?token=<token>`). Wraps `panopt-core` in a `Mutex`.
12	- `panopt` — CLI launcher and viewer. Starts `panoptd` on demand, drives Zellij, spawns agent panes.
13	- `panopt-zellij` — Zellij sidebar plugin. **Excluded from the cargo workspace** because it only builds for `wasm32-wasip1`.
14	
15	## Build / check / fmt / clippy
16	
17	`just` recipes (`check`, `clippy`, `fmt`, `test`) sweep both the workspace and the plugin. For narrower work, use cargo directly:
18	
19	- Workspace: `cargo check -p <crate>`, `cargo clippy -p <crate>`
20	- Plugin: `cargo check --manifest-path crates/panopt-zellij/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-wasip1` (same pattern for `clippy`, `build`, `fmt`)
21	- Plugin release artifact: `just plugin-release`
22	
23	Clippy runs with `-D warnings`. Pre-existing warnings in unrelated code are not yours to fix unless asked.
24	
25	## Daemon lifecycle
26	
27	`panopt up` does not restart a running daemon — it connects to it. To force a fresh daemon (e.g., after changing daemon code): `just stop && just up`. `just stop` sends `SIGTERM` twice to clear the clients-connected gate.
28	
29	Logs: `~/.local/share/panopt/panoptd.log` (tail with `just logs`). Database: `~/.local/share/panopt/panopt.db` (open with `just db`).
30	
31	## Invariants
32	
33	- **One per-project id counter (`projects.next_id`) is shared across todos, notes, agent tools, and processes**, not derived from `MAX(id)`. A `#N` reference resolves to exactly one resource. Deleting the highest-numbered item must not free that id for reuse. See `db.rs` schema notes.
34	- **Schema migrations are forward-only** via `PRAGMA user_version`. Add a new `V<n>` block in `db.rs` and an `if version < n` step — do not rewrite earlier versions.
35	- **The plugin never closes panes**, only suppresses them. Swap-in-place is the model: a suppressed pane keeps running hidden, the user owns its lifecycle.
36	- **Filesystem projection is atomic**: write to a temp file, then `rename` over the target. Never write directly to `.panopt/*.md`.
37	- **One MCP tool surface declaration**: every tool is exactly one `ToolDef` in `panopt_tool_surface::TOOL_SURFACE` plus exactly one `Tool` enum variant + `dispatch_local` arm in `panoptd/src/handler.rs`. The proxy publishes from the shared table without its own list. Adding a tool that fails to wire the dispatch arm is a compile error (the `match Tool` is exhaustive); adding a `Tool` variant without an entry in `TOOL_SURFACE` is caught by `handler::tests::tool_enum_covers_tool_surface`.
38	
39	## Style
40	
41	- Doc comments explain *why*, not *what*. Existing code uses long `///` blocks above functions and `//!` at crate top — match that voice.
42	- Errors: `thiserror` for typed crate errors, `anyhow::Context` (`.context("msg")?`) at call sites.
43	- Branches/commits are free-form. Topic branches merged into `main` is the recent pattern, but no enforced convention.
44	
45	## Coordination plane (MCP)
46	
47	The daemon exposes todos, notes, locks, agent registry, and roster as MCP tools. Each MCP connection is scoped to one project via `?ws=<absolute-path>` on the URL. State mirrors to `.panopt/*.md` on every mutation — those files are read-mostly for humans and the Zellij plugin, not a write surface.
48	
49	## Hand-launching a first-class agent
50	
51	Cockpit-spawned panes are first-class automatically (the launcher injects `PANOPT_AGENT`, `PANOPT_NAME`, `PANOPT_TOKEN` into the pane env). For a Claude Code session started outside the cockpit — or on another machine — use `panopt agent-config` so the session lands with a stable id, a friendly display name, and the bearer token in one shot:
52	
53	```
54	claude --mcp-config "$(panopt agent-config --name greg-main)"
55	```
56	
57	Without this, the session falls back to the rotating `mcp-session-id` HTTP header as its agent key. The key churns on every reconnect, locks held under old keys get reaped by the 30s idle sweep, and other agents see the connection as a series of unreferenceable UUIDs. See `crates/panopt/src/agent_config.rs` for the resolver defaults (id = `$USER-$HOSTNAME`).
58	
59	## Working a panopt todo
60	
61	When the user asks you to work on a todo (`do #N`, `start #N`, `work on #N`, etc.), call `mcp__panopt__todo_start` as your first MCP action against that todo *instead of* `todo_get`. It returns the same full detail, flips status to `in_progress`, and claims the `todo:<N>` advisory lock so other agents and the sidebar see the project state correctly. When you finish, call `todo_complete`. If `todo_start` returns `{started: false, held_by: <name>}`, stop and tell the user — another agent already owns it.
62	
63	Use `todo_get` only when the intent is to *read* a todo (browse, answer a question about it, render it) — not to begin work on it.
64	
65	## Reference
66	
67	- `DESIGN.md` — full architecture, decision rationale, data model. Read this before non-trivial changes to the data layer or MCP surface.
68	- `HANDOFF.md` — current in-flight work and deferred items.

### 6.3 Agent registry and locks

The registry tracks the agents the daemon has seen, scoped per project. Each

and `agent_list` returns every agent on the project. The first tool call on
any connection registers the agent, so even one that never calls `identify`
still appears.

Two kinds of keys cohabit, with different lifetimes:

content.text:
Done. **Scratchpads → Notes** is implemented and verified across the whole stack (todo #79).

## What changed

**Data layer (`panopt-core`)**
- `db.rs`: new **V9 migration** `ALTER TABLE scratchpads RENAME TO notes` (schema version 8→9), guarded against dev-DB drift via a `table_exists` helper. Forward-only — V1–V8 keep their historical `scratchpads`/`next_scratchpad_id` references untouched. Added two migration tests (row-survival + drift guard) and fixed post-migrate test queries to target `notes`.
- `Scratchpad`/`ScratchpadPatch` → `Note`/`NotePatch`; `CoreError::ScratchpadNotFound` → `NoteNotFound`; all `note_*` store methods and SQL.
- Projection now writes `.panopt/note/<id>.md` + `.panopt/notes.md` (heading `# Notes`).

**MCP surface** — 9 tools renamed `scratchpad_*` → `note_*` (singular, matching `todo_*`), arg field `scratchpad_id` → `note_id`. The `_mcp-proxy` auto-publishes from the shared `TOOL_SURFACE`, so there was no second list to touch. Hard rename — **no aliases**.

**Daemon/CLI/plugin** — handler enum + dispatch + DTOs; `note.rs`/`note_form.rs` (git-renamed); viewer targets, search, delete-gate, Alt-5 routing; Zellij plugin `Mode::Notes`, labels, index parsing, and the per-mode slug letter `'s'`→`'n'`. Layout `panopt.kdl` and docs (DESIGN.md §6.2, README, CLAUDE.md) updated, with the deferred **type field** (note/plan/memory) noted as a follow-up per your choice.

## Verification
- `just check` — **PASS** (workspace + wasm plugin)
- `just clippy` (`-D warnings`) — **PASS** (both targets)
- `cargo test` — **all note/core tests pass** (100 in `panopt-core`, incl. the new V9 tests)

## Two things to flag
1. **One pre-existing test failure, unrelated to this work:** `panopt::mcp::tests::agent_mcp_json_is_valid_stdio_config` asserts `command == "panopt"` but the config now emits `${PANOPT_BIN:-panopt}` (from commit `e3eb852`). `mcp.rs` is byte-identical to HEAD and has no note/scratchpad references — I left it untouched. Worth a separate fix.
2. **Rollout when you're ready:** the migration renames the table in place (existing notes preserved), but old projection artifacts `.panopt/scratchpad/` and `.panopt/scratchpads.md` will linger until swept — `just refresh` plus `just stop && just up` will rebuild projections and bring up a daemon exposing the `note_*` tools.

Note: I did this on `todo-110-sidebar-focus-hotkeys` alongside your uncommitted todo-110 changes (per your choice). `up.rs` and `todo_form.rs` carry both your todo-110 edits and the mechanical rename, so they'll need untangling when you split the branches.

content.text: Staging looks right (renames tracked as `R`, lock excluded). Now commit:

input.command:
cd /home/gregz/cp/panopt
git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
Rename Scratchpads to Notes (#79)

The scratchpad concept is durable, append-oriented shared text, not an
ephemeral scratch buffer - and "scratchpad" is tedious to type. Rename it
to "notes" across the whole stack. Hard rename, no aliases.

- DB: V9 migration renames the `scratchpads` table to `notes` in place
  (forward-only, dev-drift guarded). Columns, the unified next_id
  counter, and the tag vocabulary shared with todos are untouched; every
  existing scratchpad survives as a note.
- Core: Scratchpad/ScratchpadPatch -> Note/NotePatch, note_* store
  methods, CoreError::NoteNotFound; projection to .panopt/note/<id>.md
  plus the .panopt/notes.md index ("# Notes").
- MCP: 9 tools scratchpad_* -> note_*, the scratchpad_id arg -> note_id.
- CLI/viewer/plugin: note.rs, note_form.rs, Mode::Notes, Alt-5 routing,
  pane titles, viewer slot letter s->n.
- Docs: DESIGN.md 6.2, README, CLAUDE.md, panopt.kdl. A note `type`
  (note/plan/memory/inter-agent) is deferred.

Also folds in the in-progress todo-110 sidebar-focus edits in up.rs and
todo_form.rs that were already in the working tree.

just check + just clippy clean on the workspace and the wasm plugin;
panopt-core tests pass including new V9 migration tests. A pre-existing,
unrelated mcp.rs test failure is tracked in #114.
EOF
echo "=== committed ==="; git log --oneline -1
